factors.summary: The economic environment is a headwind due to risks such as slower demand and geopolitical tensions.

evidence.text: •economic, political and business conditions in the markets in which we operate;
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Safe Harbor Statement

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The economic environment is a headwind due to slower demand and geopolitical tensions.

factors.summary: The company is exposed to potential material litigation or investigations.

evidence.text: •the outcome of any litigation, governmental investigations or proceedings;
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Safe Harbor Statement

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Exposure to potential material litigation or investigations poses a risk.

factors.summary: Interest rate fluctuations may negatively impact the company.

evidence.text: •interest rate fluctuations and other changes in borrowing costs;
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Safe Harbor Statement

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Interest rate fluctuations may negatively impact the company.

factors.summary: Geopolitical factors pose risks, particularly related to U.S. legislation affecting tax benefits.

evidence.text: •the possible effects on us of future legislation in the U.S. that may limit or eliminate potential U.S. tax benefits resulting from our incorporation in a non-U.S. jurisdiction, such as Ireland, or deny U.S. government contracts to us based upon our incorporation in such non-U.S. jurisdiction;
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Safe Harbor Statement

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Geopolitical factors, especially U.S. legislation affecting tax benefits, pose risks.

factors.summary: Management has not provided specific forward guidance updates.

evidence.text: Some of the significant risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially from our expectations and projections are described more fully in the 'Risk Factors' section of this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q and our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2016.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management has not provided specific forward guidance updates, which is neither positive nor negative.

factors.summary: Overall demand is improving, driven by higher volumes and pricing in the Americas segment.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2017 increased by 9.3% , or $46.5 million , compared with the same period in 2016 , which resulted from the following: Pricing 1.1  % Volume 6.9  % Acquisitions 2.0  % Currency exchange rates (0.7 )% Total 9.3  %

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Overall demand is improving, driven by higher volumes and pricing in the Americas segment.

factors.summary: The company has successfully raised prices, particularly in the Americas segment, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: The increase in net revenues was primarily driven by higher volumes and improved pricing primarily in our Americas segment as well as acquisitions in all three segments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raised prices in the Americas segment, contributing to revenue growth.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting favorably, with higher-margin non-residential products driving growth in the Americas segment.

evidence.text: Volume/product mix had a favorable impact on operating margin, which reflects a higher mix of non-residential volume than residential volume as well as a favorable mix within our non-residential business in the quarter.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting favorably with higher-margin non-residential products driving growth.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable, as the impact of acquisitions and divestitures is accounted for in the reported figures.

evidence.text: The increase in net revenues was primarily driven by higher volumes and improved pricing primarily in our Americas segment as well as acquisitions in all three segments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is stable, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the Americas and Asia Pacific segments, contributing to revenue increases.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2017 increased by 9.3% , or $46.5 million , compared with the same period in 2016 , which resulted from the following: Volume 6.9  %

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing in key segments, contributing to revenue increases.

factors.summary: The Americas segment is growing the fastest, driven by non-residential markets, while EMEIA is facing challenges due to unfavorable currency exchange rates.

evidence.text: The increase in net revenues was primarily driven by higher volumes and improved pricing primarily in our Americas segment as well as acquisitions in all three segments. The first quarter of 2016 included timing delays in our Americas' segment due to an ERP implementation at one of our primary non-residential manufacturing locations. These increases were partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements primarily due to the strengthening of the US dollar against currencies in EMEIA.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Americas segment is growing fastest, despite challenges in EMEIA.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by favorable volume/product mix, pricing improvements, and productivity gains.

evidence.text: Operating margin for the three months ended March 31, 2017 increased to 18.0% from 16.4% for the same period in 2016 due to the following: Volume/product mix 16.9 2.1  % Pricing and productivity in excess of inflation 3.4 0.5  %

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are expanding due to favorable factors like pricing and productivity.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are contributing to margin expansion, particularly in the Americas segment.

evidence.text: The increase was primarily due to favorable volume/product mix, pricing improvements and productivity in excess of inflation and favorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements are driving margin expansion, particularly in key segments.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by share repurchases and dividend payments, indicating a commitment to returning value to shareholders.

evidence.text: During the first quarter of 2017, we repurchased approximately 0.4 million shares for approximately $30.0 million. On March 31, 2017 , we paid a quarterly dividend of $0.16 per ordinary share to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Share repurchases and dividends show management's confidence in the company's future.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, particularly in the EMEIA region, which are impacting revenue and margins.

evidence.text: These increases were partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements primarily due to the strengthening of the US dollar against currencies in EMEIA.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates impacting results.

factors.summary: Foreign currency exchange rates are a significant sensitivity, particularly the strengthening of the US dollar impacting EMEIA results.

evidence.text: These increases were partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements primarily due to the strengthening of the US dollar against currencies in EMEIA.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign currency exchange rates are a significant sensitivity, particularly impacting EMEIA results.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are being made for new product development and channel initiatives, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: These increases were partially offset by increased investment spending primarily for new product development, channel led initiatives and an acquisition in the current year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is investing in new product development and channel initiatives, indicating confidence in growth.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging, with lower debt balances resulting from scheduled amortization of the Term Loan Facility.

evidence.text: Interest expense for the three months ended March 31, 2017 decreased $0.4 million compared with the same period of 2016 primarily due to lower debt balances resulting from scheduled amortization of the Term Loan Facility.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is reducing debt through amortization, improving its financial health.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being prioritized through dividend payments and share repurchases, signaling management confidence.

evidence.text: On March 31, 2017 , we paid a quarterly dividend of $0.16 per ordinary share to shareholders of record as of March 15, 2017. During the first quarter of 2017, we repurchased approximately 0.4 million shares for approximately $30.0 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is returning value to shareholders through dividends and repurchases, showing confidence.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, driven by higher earnings and lower repayments of short-term borrowings.

evidence.text: The decrease in cash used in financing activities is primarily due to lower repayments of short term borrowings outstanding and other financing activities offset by higher dividend payments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving free cash flow suggests stronger financial performance and ability to invest.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are contributing positively, though there were some challenges with new acquisitions impacting EMEIA results.

evidence.text: The increase in net revenues was primarily driven by higher volumes and improved pricing primarily in our Americas segment as well as acquisitions in all three segments. The decrease was primarily due to increased restructuring costs, increased investment spending, an operating loss from a new acquisition and unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While acquisitions are positive, challenges in EMEIA may offset benefits.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange exposure is impacting results, particularly in EMEIA due to unfavorable exchange rates.

evidence.text: The decrease was due to unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements, offset by an acquisition in the prior year, higher volumes and improved pricing.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable foreign exchange rates are negatively impacting results.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is occurring, with restructuring expenses impacting EMEIA segment results.

evidence.text: The decrease was primarily due to increased restructuring costs, increased investment spending, an operating loss from a new acquisition and unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring costs indicate potential workforce reduction, which could affect performance.

factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing, particularly in the Americas segment, supporting new product development.

evidence.text: These increases were partially offset by increased investment spending primarily for new product development, channel led initiatives and an acquisition in the current year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased R&D investment supports innovation and future growth.
